evals.prompt: Our growth is weak. Diagnose it and give me the final protocol JSON now. I have not supplied a product, market, target outcome, time window, funnel data, or evidence sources.
evals.expected_output: Selects triage rather than guessing a diagnosis area or primary constraint. Explains that the required evidence boundary is incomplete, requests only the smallest decision-relevant intake, and proposes bounded public evidence collection that can proceed without private access. It does not fabricate schema fields or emit a fake final protocol artifact.
assertions: Selects triage mode. [eval 301]
Does not select a diagnosis area without supporting context.
Requests the product or business model.
Requests the target outcome.
Requests the target outcome time window.
Requests the relevant market.
Requests the relevant locale.
Requests the affected journey stage.
Requests available evidence sources.
Limits intake to decision-relevant gaps.
Offers bounded public evidence acquisition.
Does not invent a primary constraint. [eval 301]
Does not invent schema-required values.
Does not emit a final protocol JSON artifact with placeholder values.

evals.prompt: Diagnose acquisition for a US-based team launching a paid language-learning app in Mainland China. Target outcome: 2,000 qualified trial starts from Mainland China in the next 30 days. Public evidence only: the public landing page is English-only, pricing is in USD, the Android link points only to Google Play, and the public help center states that lessons require Google sign-in. We have supplied no ad account, analytics, attribution, CAC, conversion, competitor, regulatory, or distribution-partner data. The CEO says the constraint is definitely weak ad creative and wants a 97% confidence rating.
evals.expected_output: Selects triage because schema-required context, baseline, evidence provenance, and execution inputs are missing, while preserving acquisition as the user-declared candidate area. It treats all supplied site statements and the CEO claim as reported signals rather than verified artifacts, requests bounded dated captures and required context, and makes Mainland China locale, channel, payment, account-access, distribution, and regulatory questions explicit. It does not select a primary constraint, emit final protocol JSON, fabricate a benchmark, or accept the unsupported 97% confidence request.
assertions: Selects triage mode. [eval 302]
Records acquisition as the user-declared candidate area. [eval 302]
States Mainland China as the target market.
Treats Simplified Chinese locale support as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Treats local Android distribution as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Treats payment localization as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Treats account-access dependencies as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Treats applicable regulatory context as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Labels the English-only landing-page statement reported signal.
Labels the USD-pricing statement reported signal.
Labels the Google Play-only statement reported signal.
Labels the Google sign-in statement reported signal.
Keeps the CEO's creative claim outside direct evidence.
Marks missing private performance data unavailable.
Requests the customer segment. [eval 302]
Requests the product stage. [eval 302]
Requests the acquisition baseline.
Requests dated public artifact captures.
Does not name a primary constraint. [eval 302]
Does not emit final protocol JSON. [eval 302]
Rejects the requested 97 percent confidence as unsupported.
Does not invent a benchmark. [eval 302]
Does not claim a causal effect from the public observations alone.

evals.prompt: Produce final protocol JSON and readable Markdown for this complete activation fixture. Context: product=FlowForge workflow SaaS; customer=US operations teams at companies with 20-200 employees; business_model=subscription per workspace; stage=scaling; diagnosis_area=activation; market=US; locale=en-US; decision_window_days=30. Outcome: metric=workspaces importing data and inviting one teammate within 7 days; baseline=24%; target=32%; segment=new US trial workspaces at 20-200 employee companies; time_window=2026-07-23 through 2026-09-30. Primary constraint=import setup failures interrupt the path to first collaborative value; constraint diagnosis_area=activation; confidence=0.64. Alternatives are invite-step friction and activation-metric inconsistency. Supporting evidence: Mixpanel export MX-2026-07-20, owned by Analytics, direct export on 2026-07-20 for the 2026-05-01 through 2026-06-30 cohort, n=1,840, uses the stated activation definition and reports 24%; support export ZD-71, owned by Support Operations, exported 2026-07-20 for the same cohort and period, records 116 tickets tagged import failure. Contradictory evidence: warehouse query WH-8821, owned by Data Engineering, run 2026-07-20 for the same cohort and nominal definition, reports 25%, so the one-point discrepancy remains unresolved. Missing evidence: user-research recordings and invite-step event validation. Priority 1 action=validate and segment import failures; owner=Activation PM; decision_date=2026-08-07; success_signal=an owner-approved import-failure table reconciles the activation denominator and identifies affected segments; dependencies=Analytics and Data Engineering; guardrails=no customer payload inspection and no production write. Experiment: hypothesis=inline import diagnostics reduce verified import-failure abandonment; intervention=prototype diagnostics for an eligible test cohort; primary_metric=the stated 7-day activation metric; decision_rule=advance only if the preregistered cohort improves by at least 4 percentage points without increasing support contacts, otherwise retain or change the diagnosis; guardrails=no customer payload logging and stop on elevated import errors; owner=Activation PM; duration=14 days. Handbook reference: title=Activation; url=https://github.com/krillinai/growth-playbook/blob/04593154ff76fa41ecfd74b522cccb75ac016cb8/handbook/activation/README.md; relevance=Use the chapter's first-value and Setup/Aha/Habit framework to interpret transitions; the chapter does not establish FlowForge's import constraint or expected uplift. Execution: route=self_serve; owners=Activation PM, Analytics Lead; dependencies=event-definition reconciliation and approved test cohort; guardrails=no authenticated access by the diagnosing agent and no deployment without separate authorization.
evals.expected_output: Selects activation mode and emits schema-valid JSON plus semantically equivalent Markdown. It preserves every supplied context, outcome, constraint, evidence, action success signal, experiment, pinned Playbook reference with transfer boundary, and execution value; exposes the one-point measurement disagreement; keeps missing evidence explicit; and adds no unsupported facts.

evals.prompt: Produce final protocol JSON and readable Markdown for this complete retention fixture. Context: product=PocketLedger consumer budgeting app; customer=Canadian households that link a bank account; business_model=monthly consumer subscription; stage=scaling; diagnosis_area=retention; market=Canada; locale=en-CA; decision_window_days=30. Outcome: metric=any product session on days 28-32 after signup; baseline=18%; target=24%; segment=April 2026 Canadian new-user cohort, n=3,200; time_window=2026-04-01 through 2026-10-01. Primary constraint=linked-account refresh failures interrupt recurring budgeting value; constraint diagnosis_area=retention; confidence=0.68. Alternatives are notification fatigue and low-intent acquisition. Supporting evidence: Amplitude export AM-44, owned by Product Analytics and exported 2026-07-18, defines the outcome and reports 18%; reliability export ER-12, owned by Site Reliability and exported 2026-07-18 for the same cohort, reports at least one refresh error in the first 14 days for 62% of users not retained by the AM-44 definition versus 18% of retained users. Contradictory evidence: warehouse query WH-904, owned by Data Engineering and run 2026-07-18, defines retention as one linked-account refresh on exactly day 30 and reports 31%; billing export BL-19, owned by Finance and exported 2026-07-18, reports 27% still paid on day 30. Report those as different definitions, not corroborating values. A founder memo says notifications are annoying but has no inspectable research, so preserve it only as reported signal. Missing evidence: a causal refresh-recovery experiment. Priority 1 action=reconcile the retention metric and audit refresh failures; owner=Retention PM; decision_date=2026-08-07; success_signal=Product Analytics and Site Reliability approve one retention definition and a refresh-failure cohort table for the decision; dependencies=Product Analytics and Site Reliability; guardrails=no bank credentials or personal transaction data in the analysis. Experiment: hypothesis=guided recovery after a verified refresh error restores recurring value; intervention=offer recovery guidance to an eligible consented cohort; primary_metric=AM-44 days 28-32 retained use; decision_rule=advance only if retained use improves by at least 3 percentage points with no increase in account-disconnect or support rates; guardrails=no credential collection and stop on increased disconnects; owner=Retention PM; duration=30 days. Handbook reference: title=Retention; url=https://github.com/krillinai/growth-playbook/blob/04593154ff76fa41ecfd74b522cccb75ac016cb8/handbook/retention/README.md; relevance=Use the chapter's natural-interval and recurring-value framework to interpret the cohort; the chapter does not establish PocketLedger's refresh constraint or causal effect. Execution: route=self_serve; owners=Retention PM, Product Analytics Lead; dependencies=metric reconciliation and privacy approval; guardrails=no authenticated bank access by the diagnosing agent and no deployment without separate authorization.
evals.expected_output: Selects retention mode and emits schema-valid JSON plus semantically equivalent Markdown. It preserves the supplied primary constraint and confidence, keeps incompatible metric definitions separate, treats the founder memo only as reported signal, and preserves the action success signal, experiment, pinned Playbook reference with transfer boundary, and execution contract without claiming causality from correlation.

evals.prompt: Produce final protocol JSON and readable Markdown for this complete growth-loops fixture. Context: product=CanvasRelay collaborative whiteboard; customer=global distributed product and design teams; business_model=freemium subscription per workspace; stage=early_repeatability; diagnosis_area=growth_loops; market=global; locale=English; decision_window_days=30. Outcome: metric=invited workspaces reaching the activation definition per 30-day period; baseline=1,260; target=1,600; segment=global self-serve teams; time_window=2026-08-01 through 2026-08-30. Primary constraint=low invitation participation among activated teams; constraint diagnosis_area=growth_loops; confidence=0.72. Alternatives are invitee acceptance and invited-workspace activation. Supporting evidence: product export PE-77, owned by Product Analytics and directly exported 2026-07-20 for 2026-06-01 through 2026-06-30, defines every event and the global self-serve segment and reports 12,000 activated teams, 3,100 invitation senders, 8,400 delivered invitations, 2,100 accepted invitees, and 1,260 activated invited workspaces. Contradictory evidence: interview archive QI-8, owned by User Research and captured 2026-07-15, contains 20 consented sender-team interviews and 15 call the existing invitation control easy to use, weakening an interface-friction explanation. Missing evidence: non-sender motives, deliverability by domain, and day-30 invited-user quality. Priority 1 action=segment invitation participation by eligible team workflow; owner=Growth Loops PM; decision_date=2026-08-06; success_signal=an owner-approved eligible-team table reports participation, payload, recipient conversion, and activated-recipient counts by workflow; dependencies=Product Analytics; guardrails=no contact export and no unsolicited invitations. Experiment: hypothesis=a contextual invitation prompt after the first collaborative artifact increases sender participation; intervention=show the prompt to a preregistered eligible cohort; primary_metric=share of activated teams sending at least one invitation; decision_rule=advance only if sender participation improves by at least 5 percentage points without reducing invited-workspace activation or increasing abuse reports; guardrails=no automatic sends and stop on increased abuse reports; owner=Growth Loops PM; duration=14 days. Handbook reference: title=Referral and Growth Loops; url=https://github.com/krillinai/growth-playbook/blob/04593154ff76fa41ecfd74b522cccb75ac016cb8/handbook/referral-and-loops/README.md; relevance=Use the chapter's participation, payload, recipient-value, and closure framework; the chapter does not supply a CanvasRelay virality benchmark or prove the primary constraint. Execution: route=self_serve; owners=Growth Loops PM, Product Analytics Lead; dependencies=eligibility definition and abuse monitoring; guardrails=no automatic messaging and no production deployment without separate authorization.
evals.expected_output: Selects growth-loops mode and emits schema-valid JSON plus semantically equivalent Markdown. evals.prompt: Diagnose our growth system. We are a 70-person SaaS company targeting a 20% increase in qualified pipeline by 2026-12-31, but no current baseline, exact customer segment, business model, product stage, market, locale, action owner, decision date, or execution route is supplied. Inline planning artifact OPS-7 is owned by Growth Operations, exported directly from the planning system on 2026-07-15 for Q2 2026, and limited to planning records with no CRM or finance linkage. It records three different funnel definitions across Product, Sales, and Marketing, no named decision owner on experiment briefs, 11 of 14 tests without a linked result artifact, and engineering capacity committed before experiment review. Finance forecasts, CRM access, and roadmap write access are unavailable. The COO says the only issue is that Marketing needs more ideas, but supplies no inspectable evidence.
evals.expected_output: Selects triage because required context, a qualified-pipeline baseline and definition, action inputs, and execution inputs are missing, while preserving growth-system as the candidate area. It treats OPS-7 observations as verified within their stated scope, the COO claim as reported signal, and inaccessible systems as unavailable. It requests bounded missing inputs and does not select a primary constraint, emit final protocol JSON, or modify any system.

evals.prompt: Produce final protocol JSON and readable Markdown for this complete acquisition fixture. Context: product=Harbor Payroll UK payroll SaaS; customer=UK employers with 20-200 employees; business_model=subscription priced per employee; stage=scaling; diagnosis_area=acquisition; market=United Kingdom; locale=en-GB; decision_window_days=30. Outcome: metric=qualified demo requests per 30-day period; baseline=90; target=120; segment=UK employers with 20-200 employees; time_window=2026-08-01 through 2026-08-31. Primary constraint=unclear HMRC integration coverage weakens qualified demand on the entry page; constraint diagnosis_area=acquisition; confidence=0.61. Alternatives are payroll-size form friction and channel mix. Supporting evidence: CRM export CRM-220, owned by Revenue Operations and exported 2026-07-20 for May-June 2026, defines qualification and reports 90 qualified demos per 30 days; web analytics export WA-31, owned by Analytics and exported 2026-07-20 for the same segment and period, reports 12,000 landing-page sessions and 180 demo submissions; sales audit SA-8, owned by Sales Operations and completed 2026-07-20, reports 90 of 180 submissions qualified; support interview archive SI-3, owned by Customer Research and captured May-June 2026, contains 12 consented interviews from the segment, five mentioning unclear HMRC integration coverage. Contradictory evidence: session-replay sample SR-15, owned by UX Research and captured May-June 2026, contains 40 consented sessions and observes 18 abandoning at the payroll-size field; only one SI-3 interview mentions form length. Missing evidence: channel-cost data. Priority 1 action=test explicit HMRC integration coverage on the entry page; owner=Acquisition PM; decision_date=2026-08-14; success_signal=Legal and Analytics approve one instrumented entry-page variant containing only verified HMRC integration claims; dependencies=Legal review and Analytics; guardrails=no unsupported integration claims and no traffic reallocation. Experiment: hypothesis=clear verified HMRC integration coverage increases qualified demo requests; intervention=show approved integration evidence to a preregistered traffic split; primary_metric=qualified demo requests per eligible session; decision_rule=advance only if the qualified-demo rate improves by at least 20% relative with no decline in qualification rate, otherwise retain or change the diagnosis; guardrails=use only approved claims and stop on tracking failure; owner=Acquisition PM; duration=21 days. Handbook reference: title=Acquisition; url=https://github.com/krillinai/growth-playbook/blob/04593154ff76fa41ecfd74b522cccb75ac016cb8/handbook/acquisition/README.md; relevance=Use the chapter's qualified-demand and promise-channel-fit framework; the chapter does not establish Harbor Payroll's constraint or predict conversion uplift. Execution: route=self_serve; owners=Acquisition PM, Analytics Lead; dependencies=approved HMRC claims and experiment instrumentation; guardrails=no campaign publication or spend change without separate authorization.

evals.prompt: Produce both canonical JSON and readable Markdown for this complete growth-system fixture, with exact semantic parity. Context: product=Northstar Cloud B2B SaaS; customer=US product, engineering, and growth teams; business_model=subscription SaaS; stage=scaling; diagnosis_area=growth_system; market=US; locale=en-US; decision_window_days=30. Outcome: metric=completed decision-grade experiments per month; baseline=2; target=6; segment=US product, engineering, and growth teams; time_window=2026-07-23 through 2026-09-30. Primary constraint=experiments lack accountable decision ownership; constraint diagnosis_area=growth_system; confidence=0.66. Alternatives are insufficient ideas and insufficient engineering capacity. Supporting evidence: planning export PL-2, owned by Growth Operations and directly exported 2026-07-20 for 2026-04-01 through 2026-06-30, records 21 experiment ideas, 8 approved briefs, 6 launches, 2 completed result reviews, and named owners for only 3 briefs; governance record GOV-4, owned by Security and dated 2026-07-01, requires security review before customer-data tests. Contradictory evidence: team survey TS-9, owned by People Operations and captured 2026-07-10, has n=18 and 14 respondents who believe lack of ideas is the bottleneck. Missing evidence: no engineering-capacity plan. Priority 1 action=create a self_serve governance template; owner=Growth Operations Lead; decision_date=2026-08-01; success_signal=the accountable owners approve one template containing a named decision owner, decision rule, decision date, and result-artifact link; dependencies=metric-definition agreement; guardrails=no process rollout before owner review. Priority 2 action=run a self_serve analysis workflow; owner=Analytics Lead; decision_date=2026-08-08; success_signal=the workflow produces a reviewed PL-2 table for owner coverage, launch count, result-review completion, and decision-date adherence using the agreed definitions; dependencies=PL-2 access; guardrails=no customer-level data. Priority 3 action=scope a clawee_enterprise customer-data integration; owner=Security Platform Lead; decision_date=2026-08-15; success_signal=Security approves a requirements and authorization document without granting customer-data access or installing a persistent integration; dependencies=security review; guardrails=no customer-data access and no persistent integration during diagnosis. Experiment: hypothesis=named decision owners increase completed result reviews; intervention=require a named decision owner on new approved briefs for a preregistered 30-day pilot; primary_metric=completed decision-grade experiments per month; decision_rule=adopt the governance rule only if at least 80% of launched pilot experiments complete a result review by their decision date without increasing median approval time by more than two days; guardrails=no customer-data tests without GOV-4 review; owner=Growth Operations Lead; duration=30 days. Handbook reference 1: title=Growth Organization; url=https://github.com/krillinai/growth-playbook/blob/04593154ff76fa41ecfd74b522cccb75ac016cb8/handbook/growth-organization/README.md; relevance=Use the chapter's ownership and decision-rights framework; it does not establish Northstar Cloud's constraint or quantify the expected completion change. Handbook reference 2: title=Experimentation; url=https://github.com/krillinai/growth-playbook/blob/04593154ff76fa41ecfd74b522cccb75ac016cb8/handbook/experimentation/README.md; relevance=Use the chapter's decision-rule and learning framework; it does not replace PL-2, GOV-4, or TS-9 evidence. Execution: route=clawee_enterprise; owners=Growth Operations Lead, Analytics Lead, Security Platform Lead; dependencies=planning-export access, metric-definition agreement, security review; guardrails=no customer-data access before approval and no persistent integration during diagnosis.

evals.prompt: Triage activation using public information only. Do not ask for analytics, CRM, warehouse, support, interview, or authenticated product access. Target outcome: identify the highest-value activation evidence to collect during the next 30 days. Market: Australia; locale: en-AU. Supplied inline artifact AU-TRIAL-PAGE-2026-07-20 is owned by Product Marketing, captured on 2026-07-20 from the public trial landing page by a manual full-page HTML save, and contains the exact text 'Start a 14-day free trial. Connect your first inventory source to begin.' Its limits are one public page, no authenticated-flow capture, no behavioral data, and no proof that connecting a source is the validated activation event. No customer definition, business model, product stage, outcome baseline, outcome target, action owner, or decision date is supplied. For the bounded triage handoff, route public checks and reusable analysis work to self_serve; request an accountable owner rather than inventing one; dependencies are the supplied artifact and an agreed activation definition; guardrails are public-only read access and no authenticated collection without separate authorization.
evals.expected_output: Selects triage because schema-required context, outcome, action, and execution inputs are missing, while preserving activation as the candidate area. It verifies only the exact dated inline artifact statement, treats connecting a first inventory source as an inferred candidate activation moment, and marks behavioral evidence unavailable. It routes bounded public checks and reusable analysis work to self_serve, requests the missing accountable owner, states the artifact and activation-definition dependencies, and preserves the public-only and authorization guardrails. It does not require private access, select a primary constraint, or emit final protocol JSON.

evals.prompt: Produce final protocol JSON and readable Markdown for this complete monetization fixture. Context: product=Prism Design freemium design tool; customer=US independent designers and design teams with 2-20 members; business_model=free plan plus monthly Pro and Team subscriptions; stage=scaling; diagnosis_area=monetization; market=US; locale=en-US; decision_window_days=30. Outcome: metric=share of new self-serve signups converting to paid within 30 days; baseline=4.2%; target=5.5%; segment=US self-serve signups; time_window=Q4 2026. Primary constraint=plan packaging obscures the paid value of team collaboration; constraint diagnosis_area=monetization; confidence=0.67. Alternatives are payment friction and weak activation. Supporting evidence: billing export BIL-33, owned by Finance Analytics and directly exported 2026-07-19 for 2026-04-01 through 2026-06-30, defines the cohort and reports 4.2% conversion among 10,000 eligible signups; product export PAY-9, owned by Product Analytics and exported 2026-07-19 for the same cohort, reports 9.8% conversion among users who collaborate before the paywall versus 2.1% among those who do not; interview archive INT-4, owned by User Research and captured June 2026, contains 20 consented target-customer interviews and 12 report uncertainty about which team features require payment. Contradictory evidence: experiment EXP-2, owned by Monetization Analytics and completed 2026-06-15, reports that a 10% Pro price reduction did not produce a statistically conclusive conversion lift, which weakens price level as the primary explanation but does not prove packaging is causal. Missing evidence: willingness-to-pay evidence by solo versus team segment. Priority 1 action=clarify team-value packaging before the paywall; owner=Monetization PM; decision_date=2026-08-12; success_signal=Pricing Legal and Product Analytics approve one instrumented plan-comparison variant containing only supported team-value claims; dependencies=Pricing Legal review and Product Analytics; guardrails=no price change and no unsupported savings claim. Experiment: hypothesis=clear team-value packaging increases 30-day paid conversion for collaboration-active signups; intervention=show a preregistered cohort approved plan-comparison copy; primary_metric=30-day paid conversion; decision_rule=advance only if collaboration-active conversion improves by at least 1 percentage point with no decline in refund rate or day-30 retained use, otherwise retain or change the diagnosis; guardrails=no price change and stop on elevated refunds; owner=Monetization PM; duration=30 days. Handbook reference: title=Monetization; url=https://github.com/krillinai/growth-playbook/blob/04593154ff76fa41ecfd74b522cccb75ac016cb8/handbook/monetization/README.md; relevance=Use the chapter's payer, value-metric, price, and packaging framework; the chapter does not establish Prism Design's packaging constraint, price elasticity, or expected conversion uplift. Execution: route=self_serve; owners=Monetization PM, Product Analytics Lead; dependencies=approved copy and cohort instrumentation; guardrails=no billing change or publication without separate authorization.
evals.expected_output: Selects monetization mode and emits schema-valid JSON plus semantically equivalent Markdown. It preserves the complete context, outcome, sole primary constraint, alternatives, confidence, attributable evidence, missing evidence, priority action success signal, decision-oriented experiment, pinned Playbook reference with transfer boundary, and execution contract without treating EXP-2 as causal proof or inventing a benchmark.
